metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1063
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In this study authors generated antisera against different calcineurin A isoforms (alpha and beta) by immunizing rabbits with different peptides and used them to study the localization of both isoforms in different subcellular fractions prepared from rat brain. Though data showing the relative amounts of each protein in the different fractions is only presented in a table format and blots are not shown, authors claim that calcineurin A beta, similar to calcineurin A alpha, is enriched in the P2C fraction containing synaptosomes, and when this fraction was lysed, 80% of the protein was recovered in the soluble fraction, which suggest that calcineurin A alpha and beta are most abundant in the synaptic cytosol.. Experimental description: "For quantitative determination of the isoforms in tissue
homogenates from various regions of the rat brain and in aliquots of subcellular fractions, male Wistar rats (weighing 150-200 g) were decapitated, and brains were quickly removed."

The antisera was prepared using cDNA fragments containing amino acids 179-525 or 406-525 of the calcineurin A beta sequence described in Kuno et al., 1989.
